name: Dumbbell Overhead Triceps Extension
resistance_profile: Peak tension deep in the stretch behind the head, where the dumbbell's moment arm about the elbow is longest; eases to almost nothing at lockout as the weight stacks over the joint.
cues: Lower behind the head into a deep stretch
execution_steps: Sit or stand holding one dumbbell with both hands and press it overhead.
Keep the upper arms vertical with the elbows pointing forward and close together.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head into a deep stretch, keeping the elbows fixed.
Extend the arms fully overhead back to lockout.
good_when: You want to load the triceps long head at long muscle length.
Adding a stretch-biased movement to complement pushdowns.
bad_when: Limited shoulder mobility prevents a comfortable overhead position.
You want maximal loading, since overhead balance limits the weight versus a cable.
The deep stretched position aggravates your elbows.
